title: P2 regression: normalizeKind is not idempotent, so the consumer drops 7/59 real gogl entries
action: The evolving-5 P1 fix introduced a data-loss regression, self-reported by its own worker and then confirmed empirically by the orchestrator against a REAL digest. Measured: the real anphabe-gogl digest holds 59 entries; after mergeDigests only 52 survive. Seven are dropped as 'unknown_type', wiping out four kinds entirely: audit, correction, approval, closed. ROOT CAUSE: normalizeKind maps raw backlog `type` values (the ALIAS KEYS, e.g. review-finding) to normalized kinds (the VALUES, e.g. finding). The producer runs it on raw types, so a written digest already carries NORMALIZED kinds. The consumer then re-runs normalizeKind on that already-normalized value; a normalized value is not an alias key, so it falls through to unknown_type. normalizeKind is not idempotent, and the consumer path therefore rejects exactly the vocabulary the producer path emits. FIX: make normalizeKind idempotent — a value that is already a member of the normalized kind set is returned unchanged; only a genuinely unrecognized token becomes unknown_type. Do not solve this by skipping re-normalization on the consumer path: re-normalization is a D2b security control (a foreign digest may carry `kind: {}` or `kind: '<script>'`), and it must stay. Keep the closed-enum guarantee: the set of normalized kinds must be a single exported constant that both KIND_ALIASES' values and the idempotence check are derived from, so the two can never drift apart. Add assertions to tests/test_lib.mjs: normalizeKind is idempotent for every normalized kind (normalizeKind(normalizeKind(x)) === normalizeKind(x) for all alias keys AND all normalized values); a foreign digest carrying the four regressed kinds (audit, correction, approval, closed) merges with zero unknown_type drops; a foreign kind of `{}`, `'<script>'`, or `null` is still dropped as unknown_type; a foreign digest produced by buildDigest and then fed to mergeDigests round-trips with NO entries lost (producer/consumer vocabulary symmetry — this is the assertion that would have caught the regression). Do NOT weaken any of the 100 existing assertions. Do NOT fix the two open P2s (datamark's breakable guillemet fence; unbounded foreign digest read) here.

truths: A digest written by buildDigest and read back by mergeDigests loses ZERO entries — producer and consumer share one vocabulary
The real gogl corpus (59 entries, kinds including audit/correction/approval/closed) merges with zero unknown_type drops
normalizeKind is idempotent: applying it twice equals applying it once, for every alias key and every normalized kind
A foreign kind that is genuinely unrecognized, or a non-string, is still dropped as unknown_type — the D2b control is not removed
The normalized-kind set is a single exported constant that KIND_ALIASES and the idempotence check both derive from

artifacts.substantive: idempotent normalizeKind + one exported normalized-kind constant; consumer re-normalization retained
round-trip no-loss assertion, idempotence assertion, and the four regressed kinds; unknown types still dropped

key_links: the round-trip assertion (buildDigest -> mergeDigests loses nothing) is the regression guard the suite lacked
re-normalization stays on the consumer path because it is a D2b control, not a formatting step
prohibitions: Never remove consumer-side kind re-normalization — it is how a foreign kind:{} is rejected
Never weaken, skip, or delete any of the 100 existing assertions
Never let the normalized-kind set and KIND_ALIASES drift into two separate literals
Do not fix the open P2s in this cell

trace.outcome: Fixed the P2 data-loss regression from evolving-5: normalizeKind was not idempotent, so the D2b consumer re-normalization it added rejected the producer's own already-normalized kind vocabulary (audit/correction/approval/closed etc), reproducing the reported 59->52 real-corpus loss. Added export const NORMALIZED_KINDS = new Set(Object.values(KIND_ALIASES)) (single source both KIND_ALIASES and idempotence derive from, so they cannot drift) and made normalizeKind (now exported) fall through to 'return unchanged' when the input is already a member of NORMALIZED_KINDS, before falling to unknown_type. Consumer-side re-normalization on the mergeDigests path is untouched and still runs — the D2b security control stays intact. Added 4 new test_lib.mjs assertions: normalizeKind idempotence for every alias key and every normalized kind; the four regressed kinds (audit/correction/approval/closed) merging with zero unknown_type drops; a foreign kind of {}, '<script>', or null still dropped as unknown_type; and the round-trip guard the suite lacked -- a digest built by buildDigest and fed straight into mergeDigests loses zero entries across 17 entries spanning all 13 normalized kinds. All 100 pre-existing assertions are untouched; none were weakened, skipped, or rewritten.
